sections.title: Link with TradingView and Trade Master

sections.body:
TradingView alerts can send a webhook to the server, which places an order via the API with the Trade right.
This is no longer a training mode - an error in the code or in the alert formula will open a position for the entire deposit.
For a beginner: the first 3 months only alerts on the phone “look at the schedule”, manual entry after the checklist.
Trade Master in this course teaches the cycle: lesson → analysis in TradingView → journal entry. API read-only
speeds up the last step: trades are pulled from the exchange, you add the emotion and the name of the setup.
Homework: if tomorrow only a read-only key is stolen, what will you lose? Correct answer: nothing.
If trade+withdraw is stolen, you will lose everything. Adjust the keys so that the first answer is correct. When you feel FOMO, close the terminal for 15 minutes, drink water, and re-read the lesson checklist. The market will open tomorrow; the deposit may not survive one impulsive click.  Compare with Finera and personal finance apps: they are about budgets and savings, and Trade Master is about a chart setup and an honest journal. These are different instruments; Do not confuse cost accounting with a trading plan for today.  A newbie mistake is to rush with the first deposit. It’s better to just watch the chart for three days and fill out a journal without making any trades than one evening of revenge after a minus. Discipline feels boring until you compare the score three months later.  If you still have questions after the block, write them down in one line in the journal and bring them to TradingView for analysis. Learning to trade is a marathon: today’s lesson builds on the previous ones (stop, R:R, structure, sessions).

Go through the checklist once a month: (1) List of APIs in Binance - have the unnecessary ones been removed? (2) For active keys
no Withdraw checkbox? (3) Is 2FA enabled? (4) Is your antivirus up to date? (5) No keys in cloud notes?
Explaining to a friend in one phrase: “The read-only API is a camera in the store that looks at the cash register,
but can't open the safe. The output of the API is like giving the keys to your safe to a stranger.” After mastering
read-only and 50+ log entries, you can study the trade API on the testnet. Until this moment your main
“automation” - discipline and plan before the click. API security is not paranoia, but hygiene, like washing your hands. To reinforce, retell the block out loud in 60 seconds. If you are confused, go back to the markup on the 30-day history, find two examples where the rule worked, and one where without the rule there would have been a loss. sections.title: How to explain to a friend in 2 minutes

sections.body:
“The exchange gives you a key, like a pass into a building. Read-only - you only go to the reading room and look at the books.
Trade - you can rearrange chairs, but not take out furniture. Withdraw - you take out anything.
The reading room is enough for homework. If someone on Telegram asks for a key to remove furniture -
This is a thief, not an “assistant trader”. Writing it down in a journal in your own words is the best memory.”
Trade Master does not teach how to connect dubious bots; we teach you how to protect your deposit and keep honest statistics.
When a friend asks “why an API if I trade with my hands,” the answer is: “So that the numbers don’t lie, like memory after a series of minuses.” Remember: one transaction does not prove anything. A series of 30-50 log entries shows whether your process is running.  To reinforce, retell the block out loud in 60 seconds. sections.title: Tools: What Helps You Maintain Focus

sections.body:
TradingView on a separate monitor, the exchange terminal on the main one. CoinGlass - separate tab, opens
before the session, not during the impulse. Trade Master is a magazine after, not during the FOMO moment. Temporary website blockers
sessions (social networks, news). Timer on the phone “session until 14:00”. Checklist on a sticker: plan, stop, risk %,
transaction limit. Physical button “stop session” - close the terminal, get up, walk for 5 minutes. Music without words
or silence - according to the test. The main thing is the same thing every trading day: the brain gets used to the ritual and faster
enters the “work” mode, not the “fun” mode. Compare with Finera and personal finance apps: they are about budgets and savings, and Trade Master is about a chart setup and an honest journal. These are different instruments; Do not confuse cost accounting with a trading plan for today.  A newbie mistake is to rush with the first deposit. Discipline feels boring until you compare the score three months later.  If you still have questions after the block, write them down in one line in the journal and bring them to TradingView for analysis. Learning to trade is a marathon: today’s lesson builds on the previous ones (stop, R:R, structure, sessions).  In the Russian Federation, most people study through Binance P2P and USDT-M futures: the risk principles of 0.5–1% per transaction are the same for spot and futures. Don’t chase “secret indicators” from Telegram - a repeatable process is more important than another paid signal course.  In practice, open the lesson symbol at 15m and 4h: mark the last swing high and swing low - this is the basis for stops and targets. Without markings, any theory turns into fortune telling. A professional spends 20 minutes preparing and 2 minutes clicking – not the other way around.

The exchange is always open at work - the eye catches the tick, the hand reaches out to click. Mistake 3: “after dinner
I’ll sit” - fatigue, alcohol, family emotions. Mistake 4: no transaction limit - 12 clicks per evening “because
boring." Mistake 5: Comparison with chat signals during a session - someone else's input ruins your plan. The treatment is simple:
Wednesday rules are written until Monday, violation = stop trading for 24 hours without exception. and attention. At home - put away the phone, sit at the table, 45 minutes - just the schedule. At work - don't trade unless

It’s better to just watch the chart for three days and fill out a journal without making any trades than one evening of revenge after a minus. Discipline feels boring until you compare the score three months later.  If you still have questions after the block, write them down in one line in the journal and bring them to TradingView for analysis. Learning to trade is a marathon: today’s lesson builds on the previous ones (stop, R:R, structure, sessions).  In the Russian Federation, most people study through Binance P2P and USDT-M futures: the risk principles of 0.5–1% per transaction are the same for spot and futures. Don’t chase “secret indicators” from Telegram - a repeatable process is more important than another paid signal course.  In practice, open the lesson symbol at 15m and 4h: mark the last swing high and swing low - this is the basis for stops and targets. Without markings, any theory turns into fortune telling. A professional spends 20 minutes preparing and 2 minutes clicking – not the other way around.  Imagine explaining this to a friend over a cup of coffee: first in simple words, then with an example from a graph. It is important for a beginner not to learn the term by heart, but to see it on TradingView and write down the entry, stop and target before the click in the Trade Master journal.

sections.title: How to explain to a friend

sections.body:
“The neck is broken - the camp has fallen. A pullback to the neck is the last chance for sellers to enter. Distance from top of hill to camp
- the same amount down from the breakdown, if the mountain is real.” To reinforce, retell the block out loud in 60 seconds. If you are confused, go back to the markup on the 30-day history, find two examples where the rule worked, and one where without the rule there would have been a loss.  When you feel FOMO, close the terminal for 15 minutes, drink water, and re-read the lesson checklist. The market will open tomorrow; the deposit may not survive one impulsive click. □ Retest or breakout entry plan.
□ Stop above the shoulder/neck.
□ Measurement goal.
□
            R:R ≥ 1:2.
□ Size 0.5–1% risk.
□ Magazine before clicking. Remember: one transaction does not prove anything. A series of 30-50 log entries shows whether your process is running.
